description: Increased risk of bleeding when combined. Both affect clotting mechanisms.
NSAIDs increase bleeding risk with anticoagulants like Warfarin.
NSAIDs significantly increase risk of gastrointestinal bleeding with Warfarin.
NSAIDs may reduce kidney function, increasing lactic acidosis risk with Metformin.
NSAIDs can impair renal function, elevating Metformin levels.
NSAIDs reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase kidney risk.
NSAIDs diminish blood pressure control and increase renal risk with ACE inhibitors.
NSAIDs may slightly reduce the blood pressure-lowering effect of calcium channel blockers.
Calcium supplements can reduce absorption of Levothyroxine. Take 4 hours apart.
Iron supplements reduce Levothyroxine absorption. Take at least 4 hours apart.
Amiodarone increases Simvastatin levels, raising risk of rhabdomyolysis.
Clarithromycin inhibits Simvastatin metabolism, increasing muscle toxicity risk.
Azole antifungals increase Simvastatin levels significantly.
Combined use can cause severe bradycardia, heart block, or heart failure.
Combined calcium channel and beta blockers can cause dangerous heart rate reduction.
Ciprofloxacin dramatically increases Tizanidine levels, causing severe hypotension.
Fluoroquinolones increase Theophylline levels, risking seizures and arrhythmias.
Antacids containing aluminum/magnesium reduce Ciprofloxacin absorption. Take 2 hours apart.
Omeprazole may reduce the antiplatelet effect of Clopidogrel. Consider Pantoprazole.
Dual antiplatelet therapy increases bleeding risk. Use only under medical supervision.
Amiodarone increases Digoxin levels by 70-100%, risking toxicity.
Spironolactone can increase Digoxin levels and mask signs of toxicity.
ACE inhibitors increase potassium levels. Combined with potassium supplements risks hyperkalemia.
Both increase potassium levels. Combined use can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia.
NSAIDs reduce lithium clearance, increasing toxicity risk (tremor, confusion, seizures).
NSAIDs significantly increase lithium levels through reduced renal clearance.
Amiodarone increases Phenytoin levels, risking toxicity (nystagmus, ataxia).
Macrolide antibiotics increase Carbamazepine levels, risking toxicity.
Valproic acid can increase Carbamazepine levels through enzyme inhibition.
Combined serotonergic effect increases risk of serotonin syndrome.
Combined use increases serotonin syndrome risk. Avoid combination.
SSRIs plus tramadol increase serotonin syndrome and seizure risk.
Combined serotonergic activity raises risk of serotonin syndrome.
Azithromycin may enhance anticoagulant effect of Warfarin. Monitor INR closely.
Azithromycin may increase Digoxin levels through gut flora disruption.
Combined corticosteroid and NSAID use significantly increases GI ulcer and bleeding risk.
Corticosteroids combined with aspirin increase gastrointestinal side effects.
Calcium significantly reduces Doxycycline absorption. Take 2 hours apart.
Iron supplements reduce Doxycycline absorption. Separate by 2-3 hours.
Combined use increases risk of respiratory depression and excessive sedation.
Opioids combined with Gabapentin increase respiratory depression risk.
NSAIDs reduce the antihypertensive effect of ARBs and increase kidney risk.
ARBs increase potassium. Combined with supplements risks hyperkalemia.
Loop diuretics increase lithium levels through reduced renal clearance.
Furosemide-induced hypokalemia increases risk of Digoxin toxicity.
Penicillins can reduce methotrexate clearance, increasing toxicity risk.
NSAIDs increase nephrotoxicity risk with cyclosporine.
Cyclosporine dramatically increases Simvastatin levels and rhabdomyolysis risk.
Fluconazole potentiates Warfarin effect, significantly increasing bleeding risk.
Fluconazole increases Simvastatin levels, raising muscle toxicity risk.
Rifampin significantly reduces Warfarin effectiveness through enzyme induction.
Rifampin reduces effectiveness of hormonal contraceptives. Use alternative method.
NSAIDs reduce the diuretic and antihypertensive effect of Spironolactone.
Fluconazole inhibits Warfarin metabolism, greatly increasing bleeding risk.
Alcohol increases risk of lactic acidosis with Metformin. Limit alcohol intake.
Carbonic anhydrase inhibitors can alter pH balance and increase lactic acidosis risk.
Iron supplements interfere with Levothyroxine absorption. Take 4 hours apart.
Didanosine reduces Ciprofloxacin absorption. Separate by 2 hours.
Some PPIs may slightly reduce Clopidogrel activation. Monitor for reduced effect.
Rifampin reduces Digoxin levels through enzyme induction. May need dose adjustment.
NSAIDs reduce methotrexate clearance, increasing risk of fatal bone marrow suppression.
NSAIDs significantly increase methotrexate toxicity through reduced renal excretion.
Combined use can cause severe bradycardia and heart block.
Corticosteroids increase GI side effects of aspirin. Consider gastroprotection.
Combined use can cause fatal serotonin syndrome. Contraindicated combination.
SSRIs with MAO inhibitors risk fatal serotonin syndrome. Must wash out 14 days.
Concurrent use with MAO inhibitors can cause serotonin syndrome. Contraindicated.
Isoniazid increases Phenytoin levels through enzyme inhibition.
Isoniazid can increase Carbamazepine levels and hepatotoxicity risk.
Regular high-dose Acetaminophen may enhance Warfarin's anticoagulant effect.
Diltiazem increases Simvastatin levels. Limit Simvastatin dose to 10mg.
Verapamil increases Simvastatin exposure. Limit Simvastatin dose to 10mg.
Azole antifungals increase methadone levels, risking respiratory depression.
Fluconazole inhibits methadone metabolism, increasing toxicity risk.
Clarithromycin increases colchicine levels. Risk of fatal toxicity in renal impairment.
Combined use causes dangerous bradycardia, hypotension, and heart failure risk.
Combined ACE inhibitor and potassium-sparing diuretic increases hyperkalemia risk.
Corticosteroids combined with NSAIDs greatly increase GI ulcer and bleeding risk.
Ciprofloxacin may enhance Warfarin effect. Monitor INR closely.
Antacids reduce Levothyroxine absorption. Take Levothyroxine on empty stomach.
Cimetidine increases Phenytoin levels through enzyme inhibition.
Erythromycin increases Carbamazepine levels, risking toxicity.
Antacids containing magnesium/aluminum reduce Gabapentin bioavailability by ~20%.
Acetazolamide alters urine pH and can affect lithium levels unpredictably.
Corticosteroids can cause sodium and water retention, counteracting diuretics.
Phenytoin can reduce dexamethasone effectiveness through enzyme induction.
Corticosteroids may alter Warfarin response. Monitor INR when starting/stopping.
Protease inhibitors can increase Azithromycin levels. Monitor for side effects.
Amlodipine increases Simvastatin exposure. Limit Simvastatin dose to 20mg.
Bismuth subsalicylate reduces Doxycycline absorption. Separate by 2-3 hours.
Calcium in dairy reduces Ciprofloxacin absorption. Take 2 hours before or after dairy.
Soy may interfere with Levothyroxine absorption. Maintain consistent intake.
